behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Integration, because the failure boundary is request-builder -> API client handoff (module interaction), not just pure string logic. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit was considered, but the behavior text says "request sent to the external API," so a pure function test could miss wiring bugs where built labels are not actually dispatched. Higher levels were checked and rejected: no behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, real model judgment, or multi-agentic sequencing. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real infra needed, stub transport can catch it; (b) no dependence on model output quality; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no planner/tool/critic handoff. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module with a fake in-process API client that captures outbound payloads. Invoke classify with a taxonomy list and assert the dispatched request contains the full taxonomy set (no omissions, no truncation of labels list).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Integration, since the failure appears at API-response parsing + taxonomy validation + returned value handoff. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit was considered for a normalize() helper, but the behavior text is end-to-end at module boundary ("module returns 'unknown'"). Higher levels rejected: no real infra requirement, no dependence on model quality (response can be stubbed), and no multi-step agent sequence. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no real AI judgment required; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API adapter returning a deterministic out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"). Call classify and assert caller receives exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Unit, because this behavior explicitly scopes to "normalization step" determinism for identical input. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration was considered, but behavior text disqualifies escalation: no cross-module handoff is required to expose nondeterministic normalization logic. Higher levels rejected: no real infra, no real model call, no multi-step sequence in this behavior. FINAL: Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization with the same raw API payload repeatedly (or property-test with repeated identical fixtures) and assert the returned label is identical every run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Agentic, because correctness depends on real AI output behavior across live calls. PASS2-AUDIT: Upgraded to Workflow due explicit behavior text "repeated live API calls" and a sequential consistency condition across multiple agentic steps (call1..callN plus change-detection condition). Lower levels are disqualified: Integration stubs hide model/provider variability; System-only real HTTP still misses model-behavior semantics. FINAL: Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run a live repeated-call scenario (e.g., N calls) against the real provider for the same document and taxonomy. Record raw provider outputs and final labels per call; assert final labels stay consistent whenever raw provider response content is unchanged, and only allow label differences when raw provider response has changed.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Integration, because this checks config + request construction + dispatch gating interaction. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit was considered for token-budget helper logic, but behavior text includes "before dispatch" policy enforcement, which can fail at module wiring boundaries. Higher levels rejected: can be validated with local tokenizer/policy and fake API client; no real model output quality needed; no multi-agentic sequence. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess; (b) no real AI output dependence; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration dependency. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a strict per-call token budget and an over-budget document. Execute classify with a fake API client and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) happens before dispatch; if reject policy, assert no API call is made.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: System, since this uses live provider reporting over real HTTP. PASS2-AUDIT: Upgraded to Agentic because behavior text relies on provider/model-side token accounting from a real AI API call; a mock would hide real tokenization behavior and always pass. Workflow was considered and rejected by behavior text "per-call budget" for "a typical document" (single-call property, not multi-step agent sequence). FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification against the real provider with the production model and a representative document, then assert provider-reported total/prompt tokens are <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Integration, as this is retry orchestration across rate-limit handling, wait policy, and caller-facing return path. PASS2-AUDIT: System was considered, but behavior text does not require real network timing pathologies; deterministic in-process stub responses (rate-limit then success) plus controllable clock can reproduce the real failure. Agentic/Workflow rejected because correctness does not depend on model output content, only transport/status handling. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess; (b) no AI output quality dependence; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage handoff logic. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake provider adapter that returns a rate-limit error first, then success. Assert classifier delays according to retry policy and eventually returns a label instead of surfacing the initial rate-limit error.
